title: Kid's Storage Step Stool
summary: A two-step stool with a hinged top tread that lifts to reveal a hidden storage box underneath — a step stool and a small toy box in one.
description:
The two 1x12 side panels carry both steps and give the whole stool its shape and stability — build these first, since every other part attaches to them. **The lower storage box is its own small assembly**, built from the storage box sides and a bottom/footer piece, and it sits inside the lower step, not underneath the whole stool.

The two 1x8 treads cap the steps — the lower tread is fixed in place as normal, but **the upper tread is hinged**, so it lifts up to reveal the storage compartment below rather than being screwed down solid. This is what turns a plain step stool into genuine hidden storage for small toys or slippers.

Sand every edge smooth, since this is a stool kids will step on directly, and finish with paint or stain rated for the amount of handling a kid's step stool gets.

materials.name: Self-tapping wood screws, 2", or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"

steps.title: Shape the side panels
steps.body: Cut and jigsaw-shape the two step-stool side panels.

steps.title: Build the storage box
steps.body: Assemble the storage box sides with the bottom and footer into a small box that fits inside the lower step.
materials: Self-tapping wood screws, 2", or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"

steps.title: Assemble the stool
steps.body: Attach the storage box between the two side panels, then attach the lower tread as a fixed step.
materials: Self-tapping wood screws, 2", or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"

steps.title: Hinge the top tread
steps.body: Attach the upper tread with hinges so it lifts to access the storage box below.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand every edge smooth and apply finishing supplies.

unresolvedImages.alt: Kids step stool with hinged storage compartment
